rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM ROY HORSTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Lasse Lidstrom'S Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents To Albertson And Davidson, Llp, Set One, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Lasse Lidstrom's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Lidstrom's Demand for Production, Inspection and Copying of Documents to Albertson & Davidson LLP, Set One, and for Sanctions is denied. Lidstrom has failed to provide a Separate Statement as required by CRC, Rule 3.1345(a)(3). Though the courtesy copies he submitted to the Court contain a document entitled "Separate Statement Under Rule 3.1345" in support of his motion, the content of the supposed statement is not in the form required by Rule 3.1345 (c) and is instead a replica of the contents of the Declaration of Ciaran O'Sullivan. The purpose of the separate statement is to "be full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response." (Rule 3.1345(c).) Nowhere in the papers submitted has Lidstrom included a copy of the document demands propounded on A&D to which he seeks to compel further responses or A&D's responses. (Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 871, 893 [attaching responses to discovery requests to the separate statement still found to be insufficient to comply with Rule 3.1345(c).] Lidstrom's request for sanctions is denied. A&D's requeest for sanctions is also denied. The prevailing party must submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than June 28, 2016, at 4:00 p.m.
